cta.description: Schedule a call with our team to find the perfect plan for your use case
cta.text: Not sure what plan fits your needs?

children.text: A workflow run is one execution of a workflow. Triggering a workflow to a single subscriber counts as 1 run; triggering to a
with 100 subscribers counts as 100 runs after fan-out.
Pricing is based on the total number of workflow runs executed across all environments each month.
We never stop or throttle your notifications. Usage above your plan rolls into on‑demand pricing or, for Enterprise, your contract rate.

items.question: What happens if I exceed my monthly workflow run limit?

children.text: No. Billing is per workflow run, not per user or message. Subscribers are unlimited on paid plans.

items.question: Do you charge per notification or per user?

children.text: Yes. Novu Cloud is free up to 10,000 workflow runs per month. You can also self‑host the open‑source Novu Project.

items.question: Can I use Novu free of charge?

children.text: Yes. We offer reduced annual pricing and volume‑based tiers. Enterprise customers receive custom bundle pricing. Schedule a call

items.question: Do you offer annual or volume discounts?

children.text: All plans support US and EU regions. Enterprise can use additional regions (Singapore, UK, Australia, Japan, South Korea) or request custom regions/VPC hosting. Schedule a call

items.question: Which regions do you support and how is data residency handled?

children.text: Our Enterprise plan supports HIPAA compliance and BAAs. Schedule a call

items.question: Is Novu HIPAA compliant? Do you sign BAAs?

children.text: We store only the data needed to deliver and display notifications (subscriber identifiers, channel addresses, metadata). Enterprise can request custom logging, deletion workflows, or bring‑your‑own database. Schedule a call

items.question: What data does Novu store and log?

children.text: Cloud uptime SLA is 99.9%. Business and Enterprise plans include a support ticket SLA and a private Slack channel. Free tier and the Novu Project are supported via

items.question: What is your Service Level Agreement (SLA)?

children.text: Enterprise adds SLAs, advanced compliance and controls, and deployment options. Schedule a call

items.question: Do you offer an Enterprise plan?

children.text: is the open‑source core you can run yourself. Novu Cloud is our managed, scalable service with SLAs and business features. Enterprise Self‑Hosted adds SSO, RBAC, audit logs, branding removal, Helm/Kubernetes tooling, and priority support. Schedule a call to discuss which option fits your needs.

items.question: What are the differences between Novu Cloud, Open Source, and Enterprise Self‑Hosted?

children.text: Yes. Multi‑tenancy is supported with Contexts for per‑tenant providers, preferences, environments, and inbox segregation.
Most teams migrate in stages: simple events → advanced workflows → preferences and Inbox. We can guide architecture reviews and transition planning. Schedule a call

items.question: How hard is it to migrate to Novu?

children.text: Use our ready‑made Inbox or build your own with React, React Native, or any JS framework using our hooks‑only approach. Custom renderers and themes are supported.

items.question: What is included in the Inbox component? Can I bring my own UI?

children.text: Practically as many as you need. Throughput is usually constrained by your configured providers, not Novu Cloud.

items.question: How many notifications can I send on Novu Cloud?

who receive notifications through your workflows. Each subscriber has a unique subscriberId and can include attributes like email, phone, name, locale, and custom data.

Subscribers can be created via API, automatically when triggering workflows, or in bulk (up to 500 per request). They represent the "who" in your notification system—the individuals receiving notifications across all channels.

compliance ensures Novu meets European data protection requirements for handling user data.

Available on all tiers as a baseline requirement. Includes support for data deletion, user privacy controls, and proper data handling. Enterprise deployments may have additional GDPR compliance configurations based on specific deployment needs.

- A legally binding contract required for healthcare organizations to ensure Protected Health Information (PHI) is protected according to HIPAA regulations.

Specifies how PHI can be used, requires implementation of safeguards, mandates breach reporting, and ensures secure data handling. Essential for healthcare providers, insurers, and any organization handling patient data.

- Legally binding contract between data controller and processor that defines how personal data will be handled, protected, and processed. Required under GDPR and privacy regulations.

Specifies security measures, breach notification procedures, data deletion/return terms, subprocessor management, and ensures compliance responsibilities. Standard DPAs available on all tiers; Enterprise offers custom terms.

with zero infrastructure management, automatic updates, and enterprise-grade capabilities built for high-volume notification delivery.

(SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA), unlimited provider integrations, custom environments, branding removal, and priority support. Extends open-source capabilities with consumable, scalable infrastructure.
